demographics.living_situation: lives alone in a private residence

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My breathing has become incredibly tight, and I am coughing up thick green phlegm that I can't seem to clear.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Over the last two days the breathlessness has got a lot worse and is there even when I'm sitting still, and the phlegm has turned thick and green. My puffer isn't doing much anymore.

symptom_narrative.onset: gradually worsening over the past 48 hours
symptom_narrative.character: chest feels very tight; cough is bringing up thick, sticky green phlegm that won't clear; normally my morning cough only brings up white stuff
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst my breathing has been in a while — I'm short of breath just sitting, and it takes me a few words before I have to stop for a breath
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant now, not just when I move around like usual
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing much — my blue reliever puffer used to help but now it barely takes the edge off
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any little effort, even getting dressed leaves me gasping
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no chest pain, no high fever; just very breathless, tired and coughing up the green phlegm
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I get chest flare-ups now and then with my lung condition, but this one feels worse than usual
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm normally independent if I take it slow, but right now I'm struggling to do basic things like get dressed because of the breathlessness, and there's no one else here with me

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing lung condition from years of smoking — I've been told it's a chronic breathing/emphysema-type problem
past_medical_history.how_managed: puffers (a blue reliever and a brown preventer)

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: using it much more than usual lately but it's not helping much
I don't always remember to use this one regularly

social_history_volunteered.smoking_status: long history of heavy smoking; still smokes at home
social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone, no one present to help if things suddenly get worse; increasingly tired and finding it hard to look after myself or get help quickly

objective_data_offered.value: around 91% on my home fingertip oximeter
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a consumer fingertip oximeter; may be unreliable with cold hands or poor perfusion

objective_data_offered.value: the oximeter shows my pulse at about 95
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: consumer device reading, not clinically verified

objective_data_offered.value: I'm having to sit leaning forward and use my shoulders and neck to breathe, and I can only get a few words out before I need another breath
puffers are scattered on the table and I don't have a spacer; there's been smoking in the house
